title: Shadows of Meaning
storySnippet: The courtyard grows dim as Lira faces words that twist and turn with subtle shades of meaning.

exercises.prompt: Choose the word that best completes the sentence.

data.sentence: Rather than argue further, the student decided to ___ and follow the teacher's instructions.
data.definition: Acquiesce: to accept something reluctantly but without protest; to go along with.

exercises.prompt: Choose the word that best completes the sentence.

data.sentence: The ending of the story was ___, leaving readers unsure whether the hero survived.
data.definition: Ambiguous: open to more than one interpretation; unclear or having a double meaning.

exercises.prompt: Choose the word that best completes the sentence.

data.sentence: The old monastery had an ___ style, with plain stone walls and no decorations.
data.definition: Austere: severe or strict in appearance or manner; plain and without luxury.

exercises.prompt: Choose the word that best completes the sentence.

data.sentence: Spring weather can be ___, switching from warm sunshine to cold rain within an hour.
data.definition: Capricious: given to sudden and unpredictable changes of mood or behavior.

exercises.prompt: Choose the word that best completes the sentence.

data.sentence: The debater presented a ___ argument backed by strong evidence and clear reasoning.
data.definition: Cogent: clear, logical, and convincing; powerfully persuasive.

exercises.prompt: Choose the word that best completes the sentence.

data.sentence: After winning several games in a row, the team grew ___ and stopped practicing as hard.
data.definition: Complacent: showing smug or uncritical satisfaction with oneself; self-satisfied to the point of not trying.

exercises.prompt: Choose the word that best completes the sentence.

data.sentence: The young knight showed ___ to the queen by bowing respectfully before speaking.
data.definition: Deference: humble respect and polite submission to the judgment or wishes of another.

exercises.prompt: Choose the word that best completes the sentence.

data.sentence: The beauty of cherry blossoms is ___, lasting only a few days before the petals fall.
data.definition: Ephemeral: lasting for a very short time; fleeting and temporary.

exercises.prompt: Choose the word that best completes the sentence.

data.sentence: The politician's ___ response left voters confused about where she actually stood on the issue.
data.definition: Equivocal: not clear in meaning; deliberately vague or open to two or more interpretations.

exercises.prompt: Choose the word that best completes the sentence.

data.sentence: The ___ chef insisted that every ingredient be measured precisely and every plate arranged perfectly.
data.definition: Fastidious: very attentive to detail and accuracy; excessively particular.
